channel.title: Help: Drafts Critique (new threads)
channel.description: Threads in the forum category "Help: Drafts Critique" - Want to run your draft past the veterans? Post here. Please read the forum guidelines, and post a wordcount for your drafts! Wait times vary depending on how crowded the forum is.

item.title: Criticism and Review
item.description: A 782 word draft about Copyright law and **totally normal humans**.
item.content:encoded:
Huzzah! I am finally ready to begin work on my first SCP!

I do not require green lights as I already have a +1 post on the main site (here)

Here it is:
http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/doggols-sandbox-ii

Under the tab 'Criticism and Review:**

My concerns:

  • Weirdness - Is it too hard to understand, can the mystery be solved?
  • Length - Is it too long or too short?
  • Story - Is the story behind it good enough?
  • Consistency - Is the anomaly consistent?

Fair warning, this is a Department of Misscomunications SCP, so It will be a bit confusing to readers. This is intentional.

spoilers, only click after reading
hide

Basically, a group of distinctly non-human individuals are messing with the Australian legal system. They've been using it to strike money out of people who break their laws copyright, and anyone who notices they're not human gets arrested for slander. These entities are incredibly tall, and leave a lot of mess wherever they go.

item.title: Just trying to be cool again
item.description: An AWCY? format about the greenlight process
item.content:encoded:
So after spending a considerable amount of time in IRC chat, I've come to notice a few things. A lot of people are more concerned with getting their ideas greenlit as opposed to crafting a good story. This is not the case for everyone, let me just preface that, but I do see it happen a fair amount of times.

This thought process is really upsetting to me. Why should anyone else's opinion be disregarded just because they can't give you a green light? Why would you think that your own thoughts are inadequate just because you can't give someone a greenlight? Doesn't make any sense to me, yet this seems to be the thought process for a lot of new authors on site. And many of them get discouraged and leave if their first idea doesn't work out.

Anyway, the preamble is over. I'm trying to convey all these emotions and such in the third tab of this sandbox. I'm really struggling with the subtlety of this one.

item.title: SCP draft- Ancient Nautiloids
item.description: Four nautiloids from the Atlantic undiscovered for centuries by mankind.
item.content:encoded:
STAFF NOTICE: According to the Draft Forum Guidelines, please do not post your entire draft in the forums. Make a sandbox page, and keep your draft there.

If the only copy of your draft is in this post, you can recover the text by clicking the 'Last edited on [dd/mm/yyyy], [time] by [username] Show more' section found in lower right corner of this post. Then click 'Show revision' to find the full draft from before. Copy that full draft and paste it into your sandbox page. Please note that 'Show revision' does not change the post back.

item.title: The Reality Dreamer
item.description: A comatose old man who communicates with people sleeping nearby.
item.content:encoded:
http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/savanac02

Word count: 3.5k

Main Concerns:

  • The ending
  • Dialogue
  • Is the change in character warranted by the logs?

My first attempt at writing an SCP, time to overhaul it with some much need critique!

Both greenlights can be found here: http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/forum/t-13858907/the-reality-dreamer-seeking-greenlights

item.title: SCP Draft - A Sleep Paralysis Demon
item.description: Fairly short minimalist article, just over 230 words long.
item.content:encoded:
http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/penton (A Sleep Paralysis Demon)

I'm mostly looking for advice on how to improve the overall atmosphere and disturbing imagery without making the piece too long. As well as what additional details I can add for the fridge horror and just general creepiness.

Hopefully designed to be short, simple, but leave a bad taste in your mouth.

Feedback is welcome as always!

item.title: SCP-XXXX - 4D Pocket
item.description: A pocket which you can pull anything out of. Images are in the works.
item.content:encoded: no full drafts in the forum

item.title: The London Brick Incident: 3rd Draft
item.description: Article about a deadly biohazard that turns people into brick zombies.
item.content:encoded:
Critique overall would be kind thank you. I believe it is close to being ready; so I would like to get opinions on this. An SCP about a deadly fungus and brick zombies.

Word Count - 3,252

Sandbox - http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/sybert6000

Prior Critique - http://www.scpwiki.com/forum/t-13747586/the-london-brick-incident-first-draft / http://www.scpwiki.com/forum/t-13942559/the-london-brick-incident:2nd-draft

Greenlight Forum - http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/forum/t-13076170/the-london-brick-incident

item.title: WIP Safe class SCP
item.description: Asking for critique on how to go from here, what to improve, etc.
item.content:encoded:
Asking for critique on how to go from here, what to improve, etc. Wishing to make this an actual SCP at some point and post it to the site. Created this first in 2018, got back into SCP recently and rewrited most of this idea.

I moved and trimmed your thread summary, since it was taking up extra space in the main forum screen. Try to keep thread descriptions (the text in the little box under the thread title) and titles short so you don't crowd out other authors in the forum listing. ~Zyn

The idea is that the anomalous object is a living jacket that takes the skin of subjects that wear it and creates more parts to itself, like pockets.
http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/theywriter

looking to create a better background story (I have a few ideas) and also try to establish why it does and how it does it. Ive done my best to explain the SCP in a sensible way

item.title: SCP-5978
item.content:encoded:
STAFF NOTICE: According to the Draft Forum Guidelines, please do not post your entire draft in the forums. Make a sandbox page, and keep your draft there.

If the only copy of your draft is in this post, you can recover the text by clicking the 'Last edited on [dd/mm/yyyy], [time] by [username] Show more' section found in lower right corner of this post. Then click 'Show revision' to find the full draft from before. Copy that full draft and paste it into your sandbox page. Please note that 'Show revision' does not change the post back.

item.title: SCP about blood used in SRAs
item.description: A substance from an empty universe used as a reality-sink forms a relation by blood.
item.content:encoded:
A substance from an empty universe used as a reality-sink forms a relation by blood. Approximately 2500 words.

Additionally familiarity with SCP-3001 and the tale Dust and Blood (and the Scarlet King) does help explain some of the ideas in the article. However this isn't entirely necessary understand it.

Any feedback is appreciated, though sentence structure and breaking up text is what I feel like the draft might need currently.

Cognation: http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/deluchanel

Greenlights found in replies to this post: http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/forum/t-13932277/cognation

item.title: SCP "Darkhouse" - [Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded: (Posted in wrong forum section)

item.title: help fix my scp
item.description: this scp was uploaded and instantly deleted
item.content:encoded:
this scp was uploaded and instantly deleted, please help. when i asked for people to help me fix it nobody came to my aid; so before i upload it again i need all the necessary assistance.

I moved and trimmed your thread summary, since it was taking up extra space in the main forum screen. Try to keep thread descriptions (the text in the little box under the thread title) and titles short so you don't crowd out other authors in the forum listing. ~Zyn

STAFF NOTICE: According to the Draft Forum Guidelines, please do not post your entire draft in the forums.

If the only copy of your draft is in this post, you can recover the text by clicking the 'Last edited on [dd/mm/yyyy], [time] by [username] Show more' section found in lower right corner of this post. Then click 'Show revision' to find the full draft from before. Copy that full draft and paste it into your sandbox page. Please note that 'Show revision' does not change the post back.

New authors are required to get their idea greenlit before proceeding to the draft forum. Hence, this thread is hereby locked. Author, read the linked thread and follow the instructions provided.

item.title: SCP-3902 d-7623 and and the cristal skull
item.content:encoded:

Then click 'Show revision' to find the full draft from before. Copy that full draft and paste it into your sandbox page. Please note that 'Show revision' does not change the post back.

item.title: Lot of Sodom
item.content:encoded:
Hello. I'm working on another Wandsman Skip and wanted to see how people felt about it. It's also an old testament inspired Skip about what happened to poor Lot of Sodom and what exactly devoured that town.

Draft: http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/lot

Seeking crits especially on:

1) Clinical tone
2) Story flow
3) SPAG

item.title: Draft: Frozen Corpse and Nervous System
item.description: Lower half of bisected corpse revives with an apparent motive
item.content:encoded:
Greenlight thread:
http://www.scpwiki.com/forum/t-13886219/greenlight-request-frozen-corpse-and-nervous-system

Sandbox article:
http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/gopher-chucks

Sorry for the wait on this; the holiday season was busy, the audio took some time to record/edit, and I took a while to figure out the Sandbox.

Anyway, this is my first completed draft of my SCP hopeful. I'm hoping for an overall review, but a few specific things I'm hoping to hear about:
-I intended to make the skip both unnerving and sympathetic to the reader/listener. How did I do?
-I presented the series of events in relatively vague terms, and in the order of the Foundation learning about them, which is out of chronological order, so that the reader can piece together the story on their own. Am I being too obvious, not obvious enough, or is there another issue in how this is presented?
-Is the length okay? (word count is 2496)

Also, just a general question, since I couldn't find a clear answer in the guides, but does a draft need an official greenlight before being uploaded to the site, or can it be uploaded as soon as the writer is confident in it?

Thanks!

item.title: town of Alma
item.description: a draft just over 900 words about the surveillance state and some characters
item.content:encoded:
http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/www-yahoo

the main thing im unsure of with regards to this draft is the description of the summoned entity, because im sure there are way more fitting words and tone i couldve used in describing it.

item.title: Critique Request - For Rosa
item.description: Requesting a critique for my rough draft.
item.content:encoded:
Hello wonderful [REDACTED]s of the wiki. I would like some feedback on my first rough draft of an SCP. Thank you.

Draft Link: http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/romantaco-2#

item.title: Attention Craving Dog
item.description: First time SCP submission writer here!
item.content:encoded:
First time SCP submission writer here! I'm looking for feedback on the SCP I've been working on the past couple of days. Any feedback would be nice, thanks! (I also wouldn't mind some pointers/touch ups to my graphic design! It's my first time using this sort of format thing)

I moved and trimmed your thread summary, since it was taking up a lot of space in the main forum screen. Try to keep thread descriptions (the text in the little box under the thread title) short so you don't crowd out other authors in the forum listing. ~Zyn

STAFF NOTICE: According to the Draft Forum Guidelines, please do not post your entire draft in the forums. Make a sandbox page, and keep your draft there.

If the only copy of your draft is in this post, you can recover the text by clicking the 'Last edited on [dd/mm/yyyy], [time] by [username] Show more' section found in lower right corner of this post. Then click 'Show revision' to find the full draft from before. Copy that full draft and paste it into your sandbox page. Please note that 'Show revision' does not change the post back.

item.title: Proposal for SCP-5144
item.content:encoded:
http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/cedar1974

I have put my article in the above Sandbox for Approval and editing. If anyone would like to add to the Addendum you are welcome to.

item.title: Cursed Lego Set
item.content:encoded:
Hey I wasn't hoping for comments and critique on my first SCP file I've been lurking here for awhile and thought I'd give it a go!

Sandbox Link
